cases.title: Predict Binding Sites
cases.description: Given a protein sequence in FASTA format, identify active site residues and binding pocket candidates using sequence-based heuristics and known motif databases.
cases.examplePrompt:
Analyze the following protein sequence and identify potential binding site residues. Highlight any known catalytic motifs and provide the residue positions.

>sp|P04637|P53_HUMAN Cellular tumor antigen p53
MEEPQSDPSVEPPLSQETFSDLWKLLPENNVLSPLPSQAMDDLMLSPDDIEQWFTEDP
cases.exampleResult:
Active Site Residues Identified:
• DNA-binding domain: R175, G245, R248, R249, R273, R282
• Tetramerization domain: residues 325–356

Binding Pocket Candidates:
• Pocket 1 (score 0.89): DNA-binding cleft at residues 176–180, 245–250
• Pocket 2 (score 0.71): Allosteric site near L344

Confidence: High · Based on UniProt P04637 annotations

cases.title: Motif Search
cases.description: Find conserved sequence motifs across a protein family by scanning a list of sequences against known motif databases such as PROSITE or Pfam.
cases.examplePrompt:
Search for conserved motifs in the following kinase domain sequences. Report any PROSITE patterns or Pfam domains found, with positions.

>KinaseA
GXGXXGXXXXK
>KinaseB
GSGSFGTVYK
cases.exampleResult:
Motifs Found:
• PS00107 (Protein kinase ATP-binding): positions 1–11 in KinaseA, 1–10 in KinaseB
• PF00069 (Protein kinase domain): full match in both sequences

Conservation score: 0.94 across 2 sequences
Recommendation: Both sequences belong to the Ser/Thr kinase subfamily.

>Human

>Mouse

>Zebrafish
MSLTAEEKAAVTSLWGKVNVDEVGGEALGRLLVVYPWTQRFFSSFGNLSSATAISGNK
cases.exampleResult:
Phylogenetic Tree (Neighbor-Joining):

┌── Human (bootstrap: 98)
│

cases.title: Structure Comparison
cases.description: Compute RMSD-based structural alignment between two PDB structures to quantify their 3D similarity and identify structurally equivalent residues.
cases.examplePrompt: Compare the 3D structures of PDB entries 1TUP (p53 DNA-binding domain) and 2OCJ (p53 mutant R175H). Report the RMSD, number of aligned residues, and highlight regions of structural divergence.
cases.exampleResult:
Structural Alignment Results:
• RMSD (all Cα): 0.82 Å over 189 aligned residues
• Sequence identity in aligned region: 99.5%
• Aligned residues: 189 / 194 total

Regions of divergence (RMSD > 2.0 Å):
• Loop L2 (residues 164–194): local RMSD 2.4 Å — mutation R175H causes side-chain repositioning
• Helix H2 (residues 278–289): minor shift of 1.1 Å

Conclusion: Structures are nearly identical globally; local distortion near mutation site.

cases.title: Protein Lookup
cases.description: Look up a protein by UniProt accession to retrieve its name, function, organism, sequence length, and associated gene names.
cases.examplePrompt: What is the function of P38398?
cases.exampleResult:
UniProt Entry: P38398 — BRCA1_HUMAN

Protein name: Breast cancer type 1 susceptibility protein
Gene: BRCA1 (also known as FANCS, PPP1R53, RNF53)
Organism: Homo sapiens (Human)
Sequence length: 1863 amino acids
Chromosome: 17q21.31

Function:
Involved in DNA repair in response to double-strand breaks. Acts as a tumor suppressor. Has E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase activity. Required for maintenance of genomic stability. Participates in the BRCA1-PALB2-BRCA2 triple-BRCT domain complex.

Status: Reviewed (Swiss-Prot)
